quotes.text: A beginning is the time for taking the most delicate care that the balances are correct.
Arrakis teaches the attitude of the knife — chopping off what's incomplete and saying: 'Now it's complete because it's ended here.'

quotes.source: from a Reverend Mother to Muad'Dib
quotes.text: The mystery of life isn't a problem to solve, but a reality to experience.
There is no escape — we pay for the violence of our ancestors.
It is said in the desert that possession of water in great amount can inflict a man with fatal carelessness.
Many have marked the speed with which Muad'Dib learned the necessities of Arrakis. Muad'Dib learned rapidly because his first training was in how to learn.
Greatness is a transitory experience. It is never consistent. It depends in part upon the myth-making imagination of humankind.
Without change, something sleeps inside us, and seldom awakens. The sleeper must awaken.
Deep in the human unconscious is a pervasive need for a logical universe that makes sense. But the real universe is always one step beyond logic.
Once men turned their thinking over to machines in the hope that this would set them free. But that only permitted other men with machines to enslave them.
When religion and politics travel in the same cart, the riders believe nothing can stand in their way.
The convoluted wording of legalisms grew up around the necessity to hide from ourselves the violence we intend toward one another.
How often it is that the angry man rages denial of what his inner self is telling him.
There exists no separation between gods and men; one blends softly casual into the other.
Truth is a chameleon.
The willow submits to the wind and prospers until one day it is many willows — a wall against the wind. This is the willow's purpose.
When I need to identify rebels, I look for men with principles.
Any path that narrows future possibilities may become a lethal trap.
Education is no substitute for intelligence.
To know a thing well, know its limits. Only when pushed beyond its tolerances will its true nature be seen.
Government is a shared myth. When the myth dies, the government dies.

Most discipline is hidden discipline, designed not to liberate but to limit. Do not ask 'Why?' Be cautious of 'How?' 'Why?' leads inexorably to paradox. 'How?' traps you in a universe of cause and effect.
Memory never recaptures reality. Memory reconstructs. All reconstructions change the original.
Absolute power does not corrupt absolutely, absolute power attracts the corruptible.
The mind imposes this framework which it calls 'reality.' That arbitrary framework has a tendency to be quite independent of what your senses report.
When I am weaker than you, I ask you for freedom because that is according to your principles; when I am stronger than you, I take away your freedom because that is according to my principles.
Most civilization is based on cowardice. It's so easy to civilize by teaching cowardice. You water down the standards which would lead to bravery.
Bureaucracy destroys initiative. There is little that bureaucrats hate more than innovation, especially innovation that produces better results than the old routines.
The function of armies is to demonstrate the failures of statecraft.
Enemies strengthen you. Allies weaken.
All proofs inevitably lead to propositions which have no proof! All things are known because we want to believe in them.
Ignorance is the parent of fear.
The capacity to learn is a gift; the ability to learn is a skill; the willingness to learn is a choice.
It is the fate of single-minded people that, even when they hit the target, they miss everything else.
Power attracts the corruptible. Suspect any who seek it.
There is no real ending. It's just the place where you stop the story.
Show me a completely smooth operation and I'll show you someone who's covering mistakes. Real boats rock.
Every judgment teeters on the brink of error. To claim absolute knowledge is to become monstrous. Knowledge is an unending adventure at the edge of uncertainty.
When you think to take another's life, remember that your own is included.
What do you despise? By this are you truly known.
Survival is the ability to swim in strange water.
Truth suffers from too much analysis.
Hope clouds observation.

I must not fear. Fear is the mind-killer. Fear is the little-death that brings total obliteration. I will face my fear. I will permit it to pass over me and through me. And when it has gone past I will turn the inner eye to see its path. Where the fear has gone there will be nothing. Only I will remain.

It is by will alone I set my mind in motion. It is by the juice of Sapho that thoughts acquire speed, the lips acquire stains, the stains become a warning. It is by will alone I set my mind in motion.
A process cannot be understood by stopping it. Understanding must move with the flow of the process, must join it and flow with it.
The mind commands the body and it obeys. The mind orders itself and meets resistance.
The Fremen were supreme in that quality the ancients called 'spannungsbogen' — the self-imposed delay between desire for a thing and the act of reaching out to grasp it.
God created Arrakis to train the faithful.
Polish comes from the cities; wisdom from the desert.
He who can destroy a thing, controls a thing.
Small souls who seek power over others first destroy the faith those others might have in themselves.
You cannot manipulate a marionette with only one string.

quotes.source: Manual of Muad'Dib by Princess Irulan
quotes.text: Muad'Dib could indeed see the Future, but you must understand the limits of this power. Think of sight. You have eyes, yet cannot see without light. If you are on the floor of a valley, you cannot see beyond your valley. Just so, Muad'Dib could not always choose to look across the mysterious terrain. He tells us that a single obscure decision of prophecy, perhaps the choice of one word over another, could change the entire aspect of the future. He tells us 'The vision of time is broad, but when you pass through it, time becomes a narrow door.' And always, he fought the temptation to choose a clear, safe course, warning 'That path leads ever down into stagnation.'
The slow blade penetrates the shield.
Knowing where the trap is — that's the first step in evading it.
The proximity of a desirable thing tempts one to overindulgence.
People need hard times to develop psychic muscles.
Observe the plans within plans within plans.
The purpose of argument is to change the nature of truth.
Respect for the truth comes close to being the basis for all morality.
A popular man arouses the jealousy of the powerful.
Parting with friends is a sadness. A place is only a place.
In politics, the tripod is the most unstable of all structures.
If wishes were fishes, we'd all cast nets.
Let us not rail about justice as long as we have arms and the freedom to use them.
I must rule with eye and claw — as the hawk among lesser birds.
Be prepared to appreciate what you meet.
The highest function of ecology is understanding consequences.
Science is made up of so many things that appear obvious after they are explained.
To save one from a mistake is a gift of paradise.
Belief can be manipulated. Only knowledge is dangerous.
The difference between a good administrator and a bad one is about five heartbeats.
Laws to suppress tend to strengthen what they would prohibit.
Seek freedom and become captive of your desires. Seek discipline and find your liberty.
